other_observers: Brandon Percival (finder), Joe Roller, Mark Peterson.; Brandon Percival (finder), Duane Nelson, Stan Oswald, Norm Erthal, Andrew Spencer, Paul Differding, Dave Leatherman, Bill Maynard.
specific_location: Bent County Road JJ east of Road 17
description: Large empidonax flycatcher with long and very wide based lower mandible. Lower mandible all orange-yellow. Very long primary extension, 5-6 primaries extended past the white edged tertials. Head and back olive-green. Wings dark brown or black with two very distinct white wing-bars. Rear of crown with distinct “peak” (see photograph). Narrow eye-ring, slightly wider behind the eye (see photograph). White throat offset by light grayish breast band. Pale yellowish belly; undertail coverts appeared white in the evening light. Tail slightly notched and seemed to have white webs to the outer retrice.Behaviors: Joe Roller, Brandon Percival, Mark Peterson and I relocated this bird in the same grove late in the day (18:20). The bird was actively feeding on flying insects. Between "sallies" the bird would hold VERY still and seemed to disappear in the grove. The bird, while I observed it, did not flick the tail nor the wings (which is why it was difficult to locate until it sallied for the next insect meal). The bird fed "high" in the trees and was only seen once below my eye level. This bird stayed mostly to the "interior" of the grove and rarely was seen in an "outside" or "edge" tree.Call: No vocalizations heard.Plumage: Adult
photographer: Photographer: Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner, Joey M Kellner
